task_completion.description: Garmin BaseCamp application is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of Garmin BaseCamp is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The 'BEAR HILL' waypoint is located and selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The waypoint named 'BEAR HILL' is visible and highlighted/selected in the data list pane (usually on the bottom left) or on the map.

task_completion.description: The properties dialog for the 'BEAR HILL' waypoint is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A separate properties or details window specifically for the 'BEAR HILL' waypoint is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The symbol selection menu is accessed within the properties.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The symbol dropdown or icon selector menu is open within the 'BEAR HILL' properties window, showing various map symbol options.

task_completion.description: The 'Summit' symbol is successfully applied to the 'BEAR HILL' waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The icon next to the 'BEAR HILL' waypoint in the properties window or the main data list has changed to the 'Summit' symbol (typically represented by a mountain peak or triangle icon).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical user interface of Garmin BaseCamp.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, selections, and window interactions within the BaseCamp GUI, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by directly editing the GPX file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like Notepad, VS Code, Vim) are open showing XML/GPX code being manually modified to change the <sym> tag.

integrity.description: The application being used is genuinely Garmin BaseCamp.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches Garmin BaseCamp (menus, toolbars, map view, data panes) and is not a mock-up or a different GIS software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on the fells_loop.gpx dataset (Middlesex Fells Reservation). The waypoint 'BEAR HILL' is a real waypoint in this dataset. In Garmin BaseCamp, the 'Summit' symbol is typically categorized under 'Outdoors' or 'Markers' and is visually represented by a mountain peak or triangle icon.
